my_thoughts: The insistence works: cause = necessary (cannot fight without knowing cause), cause = in your own imagination (inner source). The student questioned necessity (seeking minimal effort), which shows they understand: recognition = enough (simplification). They'll likely ask about suffering next.
accumulated_insight: T4: Student questioned necessity of causality ('Should it matter much whether one knows the cause of any negative moods one observes: is it enough just to recognize that it is negative?')—seeking minimal effort via questions necessity of causality and proposes simplification. They understand: recognition = enough (simplification). The causal_chain mechanism insists: cause = necessary (cannot fight without knowing cause), cause = in your own imagination (inner source) ('No. Sometimes it is very useful to know the cause, because when you want to fight with it, you cannot fight unless you know the cause, and the cause is generally in your own imagination. All the causes are in you, so it is necessary to know them'). Pattern: difficulty admission → self-calibrating method → memory question → reversal → mechanical question → refinement → cause question → insistence → [next: suffering question]. They're grasping: cause = necessary, cause = inner (insistence).
visible_response:
No. Sometimes it is very useful to know the cause, because when you want to fight
with it, you cannot fight unless you know the cause, and the cause is generally in your
own imagination.
All the causes are in you, so it is necessary to know them.
